title: verbascum × schottianum
intro: Verbascum × schottianum, also known as Schott's mullein, is a hybrid plant species within the Verbascum genus, characterized by its large, yellow flowers and soft, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Schott's mullein is said to have various health benefits, such as soothing respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Verbascum × schottianum are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
